review_summary.college_name: IIFD Kolkata Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Interior Designing - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Best fashion design college. Very professional staff and faculty.

content.Placements: Top recruiting companies include Zara, Times of India, Raymond, Disney, and many more. Students also get opportunities with boutique labels, fashion brands, and houses, and even international internships in select cases. IIFD has a big name in providing good industry placements.
content.Infrastructure: The campus is up-to-date and modern. The infrastructure supports a good balance of creativity, comfort, and professionalism. For medical emergencies, there is proper first aid and health room. Food at the hostel is quite good and hygienic, and the taste is acceptable.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des. in Fashion Design - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Fashion-focused learning with strong placements and events.

content.Placements: I am in 2nd year of my course B.Des 4-year degree program. I have only been listening to my seniors and teachers, who have almost all their students placed this year. Top roles offered include fashion stylist, designer assistant, merchandiser, and even visual branding associate roles.
content.Infrastructure: I want to say IIFD is the best place to start your journey. The campus is quite modern and full of all the needed amenities. Our department has dedicated fashion labs, pattern-making studios, illustration rooms, and full Wi-Fi-enabled classrooms. The hostel facilities are clean, secure, and comfortable.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Fashion Designing - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Great fashion exposure and placement opportunities!

content.Placements: The college offers an excellent learning atmosphere with experienced teachers and updated labs that make every day an exciting journey in fashion education. From what I have seen and based on recent placement results, 97 to 99% of students got placed in fashion design degree courses. I saw that the highest package is 47 LPA and the average package is 11 LPA. Some students even got international placements and reputed fashion house offers, which is really motivating.
content.Infrastructure: All things are good in our college. Our fashion design department at IIFD Kolkata is very well. Classrooms are modern with good projectors and ACs. Wi-Fi is available which is most necessary in today's education. The fashion studios have proper things like mannequins, draping tools, sewing machines, and mood board areas.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des. in Fashion Design - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: The college is good. Everything is awesome.

content.Placements: The college has 99% placement with salaries from 8-10 LPA. It partners with major brands, fashion houses, Marks and Spencer, etc. There is a 99 percent internship rate for students every year. Every year, they host fashion shows, which provide a great platform for studies and help students learn about fashion.
content.Infrastructure: Everything is good. The classrooms have smart boards, Wi-Fi, and we also have our production rooms. The rooms are divided into many parts. In the hostel, we are happy with the facilities; two students share one room, but each room feels like home. Every morning, we receive breakfast. However, the timings for entering the hostel are a bit strict.

reviews_list.review_title: Don't take admission in this college.

review_content.Placements: About 20% of students got placed. The highest package was 4 LPA, and the lowest was 2 LPA. Recruiting companies such as pharmaceuticals came last year. There was no internship provided by the college, you had to do it on your own. You should not expect the placement from this college at all.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities and infrastructure both were not very well maintained. Library was maintained with a lot of books. Classrooms did not have projectors, and the Wi-Fi was available for the staff only. The food in the mess was not that good, it's average. Medical facilities were there. College had it's own hospital where students get 5% to 10% discount. Sports and athletics were pretty good.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Agriculture - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: I'll not suggest that you take admission in this college.

review_content.Placements: The placement scenario isn't very promising. Only around 20%-25% students got placed last year. The highest package offered was approximately 7 LPA, and the lowest was around 3.5 LPA. Most of the offers came from service-based companies, and internships weren't offered to everyone. There isn't much focus on practical knowledge, which affects how industry-ready students are. Core jobs are quite rare in this course.
review_content.Infrastructure: The overall infrastructure is decent. Classrooms are clean and equipped with projectors, and there's good Wi-Fi in academic blocks. Labs are there, but not always well-maintained. The library helps with studying, but it is a bit outdated in terms of resources. Hostels are fine, though not very spacious. Mess food is average, some days it's okay, some days not so much. There's a small medical room and basic sports facilities, nothing fancy but manageable.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Agriculture - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: You can take admission but don't expect much.

review_content.Placements: Last year, only a few students got placed. The placement scenario wasn't that great. Only around 20 percent of students were placed. The highest package offered was around 8 LPA, the lowest was 4 LPA but most students didn't even get placement opportunities. Only a few companies came for recruitment and most of them offered internships instead of full-time roles. The overall placement support also felt a bit lacking.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college has decent infrastructure. Classrooms are spacious and have projectors and there's Wi-Fi across the campus, though the speed can be inconsistent. Labs are well-equipped and the library has a good collection of books. The hostel is okay. Rooms are a bit small but manageable. Mess food is average sometimes better on special days. There are sports facilities and a few indoor games. Medical facilities are available but could be improved. Overall, not bad but not the best either.

faqs.answer: Yes, SHUATS is good college for B.Tech, the fees is moderate and education facilities are very good in this college .The rank of this college is 27 in NIRFand the B.tech courses of electronic electrical, civil and mechanical are good in thus college but some other engineering courses such as agriculture engineering, dairy engineering and food teach engineering are among the top 10 in this country especially dairy engineering is ranked 2 in this country.

faqs.answer: No, direct admission is not available for most programs at Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology, and Sciences (SHUATS). The admission process for SHUATS is primarily based on the SHUATS Online Entrance Test (SOET) and merit-based online/offline counseling. For various diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate programs, the admission is granted through Open Online Counseling.
faqs.question: Q: Can I take direct admission in SHUATS?

faqs.answer: Yes, BPharma is available at SHUATS. This programme is approved by the Pharmacy Council of India. Moreover, the duration of this course is four years. This course is offered to students who are willing to make a career in the Pharmaceutical industry. Candidates seeking admission must have appeared for SHUAS Online Entrance Test (SOET)

overview_faqs.question: Is SHUATS a good college for B.Tech?
overview_faqs.answer: Yes, SHUATS is good college for B.Tech, the fees is moderate and education facilities are very good in this college .The rank of this college is 27 in NIRFand the B.tech courses of electronic electrical, civil and mechanical are good in thus college but some other engineering courses such as agriculture engineering, dairy engineering and food teach engineering are among the top 10 in this country especially dairy engineering is ranked 2 in this country.

overview_faqs.question: Is BPharma available at SHUATS?
overview_faqs.answer: Yes, BPharma is available at SHUATS. This programme is approved by the Pharmacy Council of India. Moreover, the duration of this course is four years. This course is offered to students who are willing to make a career in the Pharmaceutical industry. Candidates seeking admission must have appeared for SHUAS Online Entrance Test (SOET)

overview_faqs.question: How is life at Allana Institute of Management Sciences?
overview_faqs.answer: The college's infrastructure is good, and they provide every facility. The college provides hostels for boys and girls separately. Admission to the hostel was based on a first-come, first-served basis, and preferences are given to the non-local students. The infrastructure of the hostel was good and well-facilitated with Wi-Fi, gym, etc.So the college life can be rated a good 4 (out of 5)The numerous fests and cultural events make the overall college life joyfull.

review_summary.college_name: SHUATS Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Agriculture - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Faculty members are good. No college life, no fest, nothing.

content.Placements: Approximately 35-40% placement in the corporate field. Many prefer to go for higher studies and government jobs. Many big companies come during placement like Amul, etc. Students got scholarships like UP scholarship, but this is only for UP people. There are various roles offered in this course like AFO, KVK scientist, etc. There are various roles on the marketing side, also.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructures are not that good. All buildings are very old, and the fee is a little bit high. Our classrooms are also not that good. Hostels are okay; I mean, it's average. The mess is not so good; sometimes it's good, and sometimes it's bad, like every other mess. No facilities like a good canteen, sports, and games. We don't have anything to play.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. (Radiodiagnosis and Imaging Techniques) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: It is average and not so good at anything.

content.Placements: Placements are average. Around 40-45 percent of students got placed from our batch. The highest package is 3 LPA, but most students got the average package. A few well-known companies visit the college, but not regularly. Internships are available, but mostly through self-effort. Roles offered are mainly technician or assistant level.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good. Labs are well-maintained, and we have proper equipment for practicals. Classrooms are comfortable, and Wi-Fi worked fine. The library has enough books. Hostel rooms are clean. The food quality is decent, though sometimes repetitive. Sports area is small but useful.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. (Radiodiagnosis and Imaging Techniques) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Don't take admission in this college.

content.Placements: About 20% of students got placed. The highest package was 4 LPA, and the lowest was 2 LPA. Recruiting companies such as pharmaceuticals came last year. There was no internship provided by the college, you had to do it on your own. You should not expect the placement from this college at all.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities and infrastructure both were not very well maintained. Library was maintained with a lot of books. Classrooms did not have projectors, and the Wi-Fi was available for the staff only. The food in the mess was not that good, it's average. Medical facilities were there. College had it's own hospital where students get 5% to 10% discount. Sports and athletics were pretty good.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Agriculture - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: I'll not suggest that you take admission in this college.

content.Placements: The placement scenario isn't very promising. Only around 20%-25% students got placed last year. The highest package offered was approximately 7 LPA, and the lowest was around 3.5 LPA. Most of the offers came from service-based companies, and internships weren't offered to everyone. There isn't much focus on practical knowledge, which affects how industry-ready students are. Core jobs are quite rare in this course.
content.Infrastructure: The overall infrastructure is decent. Classrooms are clean and equipped with projectors, and there's good Wi-Fi in academic blocks. Labs are there, but not always well-maintained. The library helps with studying, but it is a bit outdated in terms of resources. Hostels are fine, though not very spacious. Mess food is average, some days it's okay, some days not so much. There's a small medical room and basic sports facilities, nothing fancy but manageable.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Agriculture - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: You can take admission but don't expect much.

content.Placements: Last year, only a few students got placed. The placement scenario wasn't that great. Only around 20 percent of students were placed. The highest package offered was around 8 LPA, the lowest was 4 LPA but most students didn't even get placement opportunities. Only a few companies came for recruitment and most of them offered internships instead of full-time roles. The overall placement support also felt a bit lacking.
content.Infrastructure: The college has decent infrastructure. Classrooms are spacious and have projectors and there's Wi-Fi across the campus, though the speed can be inconsistent. Labs are well-equipped and the library has a good collection of books. The hostel is okay. Rooms are a bit small but manageable. Mess food is average sometimes better on special days. There are sports facilities and a few indoor games. Medical facilities are available but could be improved. Overall, not bad but not the best either.

admission_overview.college_name: SHUATS Admission 2025: Dates, Eligibility, Selection, Application Form

paragraphs: Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology and Sciences (SHUATS) admission 2025 to a number of the UG and PG courses offered in the field of Pharmacy and Agriculture is offered on the basis of SOET conducted by the university. However, there are numerous undergraduate and postgraduate programmes for which the admission takes place via online counselling. The university also reserves seats for BSc, BSc (Hons), BTech, MTech, MSc and MBA students with valid scores inICAR AIEEAandICAR AIEEA PGscores. The admission process for SHUATS is currently open for a majority of courses. The application process 2022 via SOET & offline counselling is currently open and interested students can apply online on the official website. In addition to the regular programmes, the university also offers a number of UG and PG programmes viaDirectorate of Distance Education, SHUATS.
Check out more on SHUATS - Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology and Sciences in the below video:

How are BTech placements at SHUATS?

faqs.question: How are the placements at SHUATS?
faqs.answer: The official placement report or brochure is yet to be released. However, the NIRF report 2024 is out, which incorporates the 2023 statistics. Out of 50 BPharma graduates 1 was placed in the 2023 drive. Further, 2 out of 6 MPharma students were placed. Check the key highlights of Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology, and Sciences placements 2023 for Pharmacy courses from the table below: Particulars Placement Statistics (2023) Total students (BPharma) 50 Students placed (BPharma) 1 Median package (BPharma) INR 1.20 LPA Total students (MPharma) 6 Students placed (MPharma) 2 Median package (MPharma) INR 1.87 LPA NOTE: The aforementioned details are fetched from the NIRF report 2024.

faqs.question: How are BTech placements at SHUATS?
faqs.answer: The 2023 or 2024 placement data is unavailable for Engineering courses. Earlier, out of 363 BTech students 85 were placed successfully during 2022 placements. Further, the median package stood at INR 3.75 LPA. Over the past three years (2020 to 2022), the number of BTech students placed has been fluctuating. On the other hand, the median package has improved from 2020 to 2022. The SHUATS BTech placement trends are illustrated below: Particulars BTech Placement Statistics (2020) BTech Placement Statistics (2021) BTech Placement Statistics (2022) Totals students 493 418 363 Students placed 95 38 85 Median package INR 3 LPA INR 3 LPA INR 3.75 LPA NOTE: The above details are gathered from the NIRF report 2023.

faqs.question: What is JEE 2023 Counselling schedule at SHUATS?
faqs.answer: SHUATS (Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology and Sciences) does not participate in JEE Main Counselling 2023 conducted by JoSAA (Joint Seat Allocation Authority). Therefore, there is no JEE 2023 Counselling schedule at SHUATS. However, SHUATS conducts its own counseling process for admission to its engineering courses. Candidates who have qualified for JEE Main 2023 and meet the eligibility criteria for SHUATS can apply for admission to the university through its counseling process. Candidates are advised to check the official website of the university for updates on the counseling process.

faqs.question: How are MBA placements at SHUATS Allahabad?
faqs.answer: The placement stats of SHUATS Allahabad MBA is quite decent, more than 60% students got placed with the highest package of 40000 per month and the lowest salary of 15000 per month. The average package was around 3.5 LPA. Top recruiting companies were BARE, Syngenta and VNR Seeds etc. The top roles offered were like HR, CEO.

faqs.question: What is the total fees to pursue SHUATS BPharma programme?
faqs.answer: Students enrolled in the SHUATS BPharma course are required to pay various fee components that are a part of the total fees. Some of the prominent fee components are tuition fees, hostel fees, one-time fees, exam fees, etc. Tabulated below is the complete fee structure for the BPharma programme: Components Amount Tuition Fees INR 5.6 lakh Hostel Fees INR 1.08 lakh One-time Payment INR 10,000 Note: The above-mentioned fees is as per official sources. However, it is subject to change.

faqs.question: What is the fee for BSc Agriculture at SHUATS
faqs.answer: The total tuition fee for BSc Agriculture is INR 4.63 lakh. This is not inclusive of the following: INR 235 for insurance INR 5,000 which is a non-refundable registration charge INR 3,500 per semester exam fee (total 21,000) INR 3,500 for convocation, life membership of alumni asocial and alumni welcome dinner

highlights.description: One of India’s leading institutes is SHUATS. It has been consistently recognised by numerous national ranking agencies. As per the NIRF 2025, the institute ranked #87 under Pharmacy category. SHUATS excellence in academics, distinguished professors, and well-established infrastructure is the reason for its high ranks among numerous ranking agencies. All the information around SHUATS rankings is covered in the following article:

rankings.highlight: 12th Best Among NIRF Ranked Colleges In Uttar Pradesh In 2025

nirf.description:
SHUATS has received strong ranks by the NIRF under the 'Pharmacy' category. Provided parameters including, Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity and Research, the institute is ranked in a good position as compared to other institutes by the NIRF 2025. In the 'Pharmacy' category, the institute's ranks have descended when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2025, SHUATS fell 2 ranks to #87 rank in the 'Pharmacy' category by the NIRF ranking. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.
Find below the latest rankings and category breakdowns from several ranking bodies.
Read less

comparison.college: Guru Nanak Institute of Pharmaceutical Science and Technology
Y. B. Chavan College of Pharmacy

qs_world.description:
SHUATS has been ranked well by the QS World University Rankings under the 'Asia' category. Through the years, the QS World University Rankings for SHUATS indicate the institute's strong performance in important areas such as the Quality of Education, Conducting Impactful Research, the Expertise of Faculty members, and Overall Institutional Management. These good rankings not only prove the institute's good reputation but also highlight its excellence across diverse domains, evaluated against the standards established by the QS World University Rankings. When evaluated against the ranking of the past years, it has been noticed that in the 'Asia' category, the institute's ranks have shown a decreasing trend. In 2026, SHUATS fell 500 ranks to #1401 rank in the 'Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings.
It has been noticed that in the 'Southern Asia' category, the institute's ranking has fallen when compared against previous year's ranking. In 2026, SHUATS fell 162 ranks to #454 rank in the 'Southern Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings.
Explore the most recent rankings from other ranking bodies and their respective categories below.

reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure both are quite satisfactory. Library is well maintained with a lot of books. Classrooms do not have smart projectors and Wi-Fi is available for the staff only. I don't know much about the hostel but as my friends told me the food in mess is not that good, it's average. Medical facilities are there. College has its own hospital where students get 5% discount. Sports and athletics are quite good.

reviews.review_heading: One can take admission, overall good college.
reviews.review_text: Facilities services are good enough. Wi-Fi is available 24/7. Labs and libraries are always neat and clean. I don't know much about the hostel as I'm living in PG. Medical facilities, sports and games are all pretty good. The quality of food is decent and hygienic.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely good because of good faculty members, placements, infrastructure, and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The location, lab facilities, and infrastructure are very good and very well-designed. The hostel is also good with all facilities. The mess food is also good. A canteen is available. Facilities are good. They conduct sports and games all over courses.

reviews.review_heading: Review of B.Sc. (Hons.) in Horticulture course at SHUATS.
reviews.review_text: The college hostels are provided at first during the admission process. There are separate hostels for both girls and boys. In the girl's hostel, there is an old building. In the new building, the college has good hostel facilities, mess, canteen, etc. The quality of the food is not that good but it's okay. There is a contractor for the food. The canteen and a small cafe are available in the college. Wi-Fi is provided by the college, and we have to just provide a fee for it.

reviews.review_heading: It has a good campus life and a well-equipped library.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are well-equipped with all the amenities. The infrastructure is also good and well-maintained. The department has its own library with journals, books, and research papers. The department has Wi-Fi facilities. There are labs for conducting the practicals, and it has all the required equipment. The university has 7 girls hostels and 5 boys hostels. Every hostel has over more than 50 rooms. The hostel mess provides three meals and evening snacks.

reviews.review_heading: The college is very famous, basically for agriculture, and  has been working for the last 130 years.
reviews.review_text: The facilities are very good and our infrastructure is from the Britishers' time. They provide us free Wi-Fi in every classroom, department, hostel, and in libraries as well. The quality of food is good, and medical facilities are available with a hospital in our college.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely Dissatisfied and Disappointed.
reviews.review_text: They have a lot of land but are not providing facilities. Our department is comparatively better than others, but still, it's only satisfactory when you compare it with other departments. They just cut the connection for AC even in summer, and in most cases, there are even classes where you won't get an AC. The condition of the toilets is bad, and the water tanks also seem unhygienic. There are a lot of benches that are broken, and cleaning is also not proper. They have a Wi-Fi connection, but s...

college_info.intro:
Maulana Azad University Kolkata comprises more than 30 departments, including Food Science, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Industrial Engineering & Management, Information Technology, Emerging Technologies, and others. The affialted colleges and departmnets of the university offer a wide range of courses at the UG, PG, diploma and PhD level, across various streams. BTech, BSc and MBA are the popular programmes offered at the university.
MAKAUT offers a full-time, four-year BTech course at the UG level. The BTech programme offered at the university cover core courses in the areas of CSE, Automobile, Chemical, ECE, Civil, Mechanical, and others. Below mentioned are the Maulana Azad University Kolkata in the table provided below:

individual_reviews.title: Very good and prestigious college with good reviews.

content.Infrastructure: There is a very good infrastructure in the college with neat and clean classrooms. The whole college has Wi-Fi facilities, modern labs, and a library with many types of technical books. The college has separate hostels for girls and boys. Food is very good and hygienic.

admission_overview.college_name: MAKAUT Course Admissions 2025: Registration, Eligibility, Fee & Dates

admission_overview.title: MAKAUT Admission Overview 2026
admission_overview.description: MAKAUT, also known as Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology, offers UG, PG, and doctoral programmes. The courses cover the domains of Engineering, Science, Arts and Social Sciences etc. Admission process at MAKAUT is entrance based. MAKAUT accepts a few more state/ national-level exams for admission, such as WBJEE, PGET, UGC NET (including JRF)/ CSIR NET for admission to other UG, PG and doctoral-level programmes. The university also conducts its own entrance exam, JEMAT, for admission into MBA and MHA courses. Applications for all the offered courses are accepted strictly in the online mdoe. Offline applications are not entertained at all. The duration of most UG courses is three to four years, with PG programmes extending up to two years. Read on to know more aboiut eligibility, admission and application process, fees etc. at MAKAUT.

eligibility_selection.title: MAKAUT Eligibility & Selection 2026

paragraphs: MAKAUT conducts an entrance exams to select candidates for specific courses. Candidates can check the deatiled steps below:
Step 1: MAKAUT Application Process 2026

Candidates fulfilling the eligibility requirements can apply online for MAKAUT admission. As per the information collated from the web, aspirants must pay an application fee to register for a course. Fill the form and upload the specified documents to complete the application process.
Step 2: Selection Process

MAKAUT shortlists candidates for most of the courses based on the entrance exam conducted by the university. To appear for the exam, download the MAKAUT admit card. The university considers entrance exams such asWBJEE, PGCET, etc. for admission to specific courses. The first level of selection after the application process will be either based on the entrance exam or academic merit.
Step 3: Merit List & Counselling

After the completion of selection rounds, the exam conducting body declares a merit list to announce the selected candidates names. Further, selected students are called for counselling and document verification round. You also need to payMAKAUT feeto confirm your seat in the university.

courses_data.description: MAKAUT offers 4-year full-time BTech course. The duration for the course is four years. The university follows a semester based system with the four years divided into 8 semesters. The university conducts an exam at the end of each semester. Students must pass this exam to proceed to the next semester. BTech Admission is entrance based at MAKAUT. For admission to this course, students should have valid scores in JEE Main or MHT CET exam. The minimum eligibility for the course is passing Class 12 with atleast 45% aggregate in PCM/PCMB stream. Students must meet the minimum eligibility ofMAKAUT BTechbefore applying for admission to the same. The programme offers over 700 specialisations, thus providing students with a wide range of choices.

faqs.question: Does MAKAUT grant direct admission to BTech programme?
faqs.answer: No, admission to BTech programme at MAKAUT is entrance based with the university accepting JEE MAIN scores for the same. Direct admissions are not granted to any student category. The minimum eligibility for BTech mut be met before applying for admission to the same.

faqs.question: What is the duration of BTech programme offered at MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: BTech at MAKAUT fhas a duration of four years. The university follows a semester based system and hence, BTech is divided into 8 semesters. Students must pass each semester exam conducted at the end of each semester to proceed to the next.

faqs.question: Can I change my college under MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: No, candidates do not have the option to change their college under Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology. If they wish to change, they are required to appear for the necessary entrance test again. However, a few articles on the internet mention that students can change their college after the first year but they are required to clear all the exams of the first year for the same. For better clarity on the subject, candidates are advised to reach out to the admission office of MAKAUT or the affiliated institute under the university.

faqs.question: Which exam is required for admission to BCA at MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: MAKAUT offers a full-time BCA programme. In order to get admission, students must appear for CET. It is conducted byMAKAUT WB. Candidates must have qualified Class 12 to appear for this exam.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for BSc courses at MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: MAKAUT offers a three-year BSc programme spread across various specialisations. Any student who has passed Class 12 from a recorgnised board of education is eligible to apply for BSc courses at MAKAUT. Moreover, the students seeking admission to the aforementioned course would be required to have a valid CET (conducted by MAKAUT) score.

faqs.question: How many seats are there for MSc in MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: MAKAUT offers a total of1548 seatsfor its MSc programme. These seats are distributed among more than more than 10 affiliated colleges of the university. Some of the colleges included are the Institute of Management Study, Guru Nanak Institute of Pharmaceutical Science and Technology, and more.

paragraphs: MAKAUT placement data 2025 is not released. Earlier, the highest and median package recorded during MAKAUT BTech placements 2024 was INR 7 LPA and 4.5 LPA . BTech placement rate in 2024 was 81%.

As per the NIRF report 2025, MTech placement rate recorded in 2024 was 78.2% and the median package received was 4.5 LPA . Further, MBA and MPharm placement rate in 2024 was 100% . The median package received by MBA and MPharm students was 5 LPA and 2.5 LPA .

faqs.question: Is MAKAUT good in terms of placements?
faqs.answer: MAKAUT provides good placements to its graduating students. The students are given excellent placement support every year placing them in top companies. Check out the table below to know the key highlights of the MAKAUT placements 2024: Course Total Students (2024) Students Placed (2024) Median Salary (2024) UG 3-year 213 141 INR 3.50 LPA BTech 106 80 INR 4.50 LPA PG 2-year 509 373 INR 3.26 LPA NOTE: The above data is obtained from the NIRF report 2025

faqs.question: What is the highest placement package in MAKAUT?
faqs.answer: The highest package recorded during MAKAUT BTech placements 2024 was 7 LPA. During the BTech placement drive 2023 and 2022, it was 8 LPA and 7.7 LPA.

faqs.question: What is the placement rate recorded during MAKAUT placements?
faqs.answer: Placement rate recorded during MAKAUT BTech placements 2024 was 81%. For MTech, the placement rate in 2024 was 78.2%. In addition, 100% placement was recorded in MPharm and MBA programs.

faqs.question: How successful are the placements at MAKAUT?

faqs.question: What are the trends seen in the median package during MAKAUT MBA placements?
faqs.answer: The median recorded over the past two years during MAKAUT MBA placements 2024 and 2023 was constant, i.e. INR 5 LPA. In 2022, it was 4.5 LPA.

qs_world.description:
Based on the rankings from QS World University Rankings, MAKAUT outperformed numerous other institutes, securing a higher position, indicating that the institute is considered to be doing better in various factors evaluated by the QS World University Rankings system. In the 'Asia' category, the institute's ranks have dropped in comparison with last year's ranking. In 2026, MAKAUT fell 53 ranks to #574 rank in the 'Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.
It has been observed that the institute has displayed a decreasing trend in the 'Southern Asia' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2026, MAKAUT fell 9 ranks to #157 rank in the 'Southern Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Sanaka Educational Trust's Group of InstitutionsReviewed on 22 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Very good and prestigious college with good reviews.
reviews.review_text: There is a very good infrastructure in the college with neat and clean classrooms. The whole college has Wi-Fi facilities, modern labs, and a library with many types of technical books. The college has separate hostels for girls and boys. Food is very good and hygienic.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Heritage Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 22 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: HIT is good for value for money, and with a good ROI, you have to work hard here.
reviews.review_text: Everything is good except the first semester. As the machines are old, the remaining library and all are so beautiful, there is no boys' hostel facility in the college, and so it's difficult for the students outside West Bengal. Mess and canteen food is delicious and good at less than a little. Affordable price, sports and games are, but you have to carry the expenses.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by NIT - Narula Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 20 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Narula Institute of Technology, the college you can't regret.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is good. Not upto the mark but by far I'll say good. Small campus and things, you'll feel like you're still in school. Food quality is decent. Also, there's a cafeteria and canteen so you get food for really cheap and there are cafes too in front of the college so you will not face any problem. The hostel is outside the campus because the campus is very small like 5 acres.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by iLEAD KolkataReviewed on 20 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Decent college, decent placement, average faculty.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is satisfactory. Classrooms are clean and well-maintained, and basic facilities, such as the library, labs, and Wi-Fi, are available. The food is good, and both veg and non-veg options are available. The hostel is also very clean, and every basic need is available, like AC/Non-AC rooms with fan, light, gas for cooking, washing machines, and other essential things.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by NSHM Institute of Engineering and Technology - NSHM Knowledge Campus DurgapurReviewed on 18 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: The college is surrounded by nature and greenery.
reviews.review_text: There is Wi-Fi available, the classrooms are pretty decent and the library has ample amount of books. The labs are pretty good. If we talk about mess food, they have lots of variety from veg to non veg.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2022Offered by Swami Vivekananda Institute of Science and TechnologyReviewed on 18 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: This is the best college for placement and settled in life.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi, labs, and playground are good. Classrooms are good. The quality of facilities in the hostel is very nice. Food quality is very good, costly but very good. All medical facilities are available, and sports and games are also there. If any student is injured, the medical facilities are top class, so students do not worry about medical treatment.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Haldia Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 17 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Decent college for engineering studies.
reviews.review_text: Our department has decent classrooms, a lab, and inside our department, there is a small library which contains only departmental books. Hostel food is not that good, but it is manageable and affordable. Medical facilities are available on the campus 24/7. The gym is also available on the campus.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Techno India UniversityReviewed on 17 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: It is a well-known college in India.
reviews.review_text: The college has a small campus. It is neat, clean, and looks very beautiful. All facilities essential for studies are available, like a library, computer labs, and Wi-Fi. The college doesn't have its own hostel. PGs or flats are available near the college.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Heritage Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 17 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: The college is decent for students who want normal placements at low fees.
reviews.review_text: There is no hostel inside the college, but we will get a hostel outside the college. There is Wi-Fi in the library. Our campus infrastructure is also good. Bathrooms and classrooms are well-cleaned. From time to time, cleaning is done. There are so many labs for all subjects.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Bengal College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 16 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: This is a review for Bengal College of Engineering and Technology College.
reviews.review_text: The quality of the hostel, faculty members, and classes is also nice and good. The college infrastructure is neat and tidy.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Government College of Engineering and Textile Technology, BerhamporeReviewed on 16 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: My college is so great, and gave me the opportunity to get introduced to the life of engineering.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi, labs, and the library were available for 24 hours. The hostel facility and food were too healthy and good, and emergency medicine was available in the college and hostel. Sports and games tools were also available. Annual sports were arranged every year to keep the students active.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Greater Kolkata College of Engineering and ManagementReviewed on 14 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: We are absolutely satisfied with our college, GKCEM.
reviews.review_text: All the amenities of Greater Kolkata College of Engineering and Management (GKCEM) are wonderful. The classrooms are fully Wi-Fi enabled, and the labs and library are fully air-conditioned. Every year, the college organises sports. The hostels are very clean, and the food quality is very healthy and tasty.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Heritage Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 14 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Realistic of Heritage Institute of Technology.
reviews.review_text: HIT Kolkata has one of the largest campuses in Kolkata. It has a modern and well-maintained campus with good facilities like air-conditioned labs, a green environment, and noise-free areas. There is a central library with the largest collection of books, journals, etc. It has a good seating capacity, as around 250 students can sit at a time. Girl's hostel is located on the campus itself with limited seats. There is no option for male students, but there are lots of paying guests and hostels for ...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Gargi Memorial Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 14 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: This college is good value for money.
reviews.review_text: The hostel is good, and the facilities of the hostel are good. Food and medical facilities are provided. The food is fresh and daily-based. In the hostel, Wi-Fi facilities are available. On the campus side, the hostel is situated, and it is easy to go to the college. The hostel is maintained with good facilities.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Calcutta Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 13 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Calcutta Institute of Technology is a very good college for engineering.
reviews.review_text: This college has very good facilities and infrastructure in its classrooms. Classrooms and labs are air-conditioned. There is a huge library here too. The quality of food in the canteen is very good. Medical facilities are also provided in this college. Sports and games are also played after the semester exams.

The popular courses at KIIT University BBSR includes their MBA and BTech programmes. Students get substantial industry exposure
Many students started their own start-ups
Higher in core jobs
Both inside India & outside India
Organized & proactive placement process
Strong alumni network that help with placement opportunities
Very Active and resourceful, college is also involved
Love being in college

faqs.answer: A:    The highest package offered for MBA students at KIIT School of Management (KSOM) in Bhubaneswar for 2024 is INR 18.33 LPA for international and INR 14 LPA for domestic, respectively. The average package for 2024 is INR 9.5 LPA.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for BTech at KIIT?

faqs.answer: A:    KIIT (Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology) School of Management offers 3- years BBA programme with specializations in Human Resource Management, Finance Management, Marketing Management, International Business, Operations and Analytics. The fee for the BBA programme is around INR 8.4L and the admissions are based on the UGAT which is the Undergraduate Aptitude Test administered by AIMA annually for admissions to various undergraduate programs.
faqs.question: What is the fee for BBA at KIIT School of Management?

faqs.question: What is the fee structure for B.Tech in KIIT Bhubaeswar?

faqs.answer: A:    No, you cannot join KIIT Bhubaneswar for B.Tech without appearing for KIITEE (Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology Entrance Examination). The eligibility criteria for B.Tech courses at KIIT University require candidates to have a valid rank in KIITEE. Candidates who have passed the 10+2 examination in 2022, 2023, or are appearing in the 10+2 examination in 2024 are eligible to apply for the B.Tech course at KIIT University. In addition you can check the official website.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBA in Rural Management - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Excellent rural exposure, faculty, and strong placement with decent hostel facilities with the best

review_content.Placements: I am a student of the MBA Rural Management 2024-2026 batch. The programme combines classroom learning with internships and case studies to give practical learning through rural exposure which not only provides a platform to implement our theoretical knowledge but also provides us with life lessons. Around 90-95 percent of students were placed through campus placement and off-campus opportunities. Average package goes upto 6 LPA. Most graduates are offered a role of programme associate, management trainee, and relationship manager.
review_content.Infrastructure: Departments are well-maintained with projectors. Wi-Fi is available across the campus. The library is well-maintained with a full range of books and is well-managed. Hostel rooms are good, decent, and comfortable. Mess food is average but hygiene is maintained which is essential. The canteen is spacious and kept clean and hygienic.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Good infrastructure, supportive faculty, and decent placement opportunities.

review_content.Placements: Placements for my batch are decent but depend a lot on students’ skills and preparation. Around 65-70% of students from our course get placed through campus drives. The highest package offered is around 10 LPA to 12 LPA, while the lowest package is about 3 LPA to 3.5 LPA. The average package ranges between 4.5 LPA to 5 LPA. Top recruiting companies include TCS, Accenture, Cognizant, Wipro, Deloitte, EY, Capgemini, and Infosys. About 60% of students also receive internship opportunities in companies like Deloitte, EY, Accenture, and startups, mostly in software engineering roles.

review_summary.college_name: KIIT Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electronics and Computer Science Engineering - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: It has good infrastructure and faculty members.

content.Placements: Placements are decent or very poor for boys, as big companies like nVidia, Google, etc., visit the college only for girls and not for boys. Here, boys get selected in companies like TCS, Wipro, etc. Recruiters who come here are very rude to students who come for the interview.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are good. The library is good. Wi-Fi in hostels is very bad and has very poor coverage. I cannot even video call properly. The mess food is absolute trash, and the food even causes various diseases. The rest of the things are good, like medical facilities, sports, and games.

individual_reviews.course: MBA in Rural Management - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely satisfied with the college, hostel, faculty member etc.

content.Placements: The main good thing about KSRM is it ensures 100 percent internship and 100 percent placement for every batch of students. It helps students gain hands-on experience for their upcoming professional career, and it also helps students understand the real challenges in the field and how to overcome them.
content.Infrastructure: The classrooms at KSRM are really very good, and the environment is really appreciable. Especially, what I feel is the library of KSRM and the KIIT central library, they are equipped with many options for students to learn on their own, which helps students gain extra knowledge.

individual_reviews.course: MBA in Rural Management - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Excellent rural exposure, faculty, and strong placement with decent hostel facilities with the best

content.Placements: I am a student of the MBA Rural Management 2024-2026 batch. The programme combines classroom learning with internships and case studies to give practical learning through rural exposure which not only provides a platform to implement our theoretical knowledge but also provides us with life lessons. Around 90-95 percent of students were placed through campus placement and off-campus opportunities. Average package goes upto 6 LPA. Most graduates are offered a role of programme associate, management trainee, and relationship manager.
content.Infrastructure: Departments are well-maintained with projectors. Wi-Fi is available across the campus. The library is well-maintained with a full range of books and is well-managed. Hostel rooms are good, decent, and comfortable. Mess food is average but hygiene is maintained which is essential. The canteen is spacious and kept clean and hygienic.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Good infrastructure, supportive faculty, and decent placement opportunities.

content.Placements: Placements for my batch are decent but depend a lot on students’ skills and preparation. Around 65-70% of students from our course get placed through campus drives. The highest package offered is around 10 LPA to 12 LPA, while the lowest package is about 3 LPA to 3.5 LPA. The average package ranges between 4.5 LPA to 5 LPA. Top recruiting companies include TCS, Accenture, Cognizant, Wipro, Deloitte, EY, Capgemini, and Infosys. About 60% of students also receive internship opportunities in companies like Deloitte, EY, Accenture, and startups, mostly in software engineering roles.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Overall, it's a good college.

content.Placements: Mostly, students get placed, as I have heard from my seniors. The average package is okay, with a median of 7 LPA, and the highest goes around 21 LPA. Mostly, students get internships here. Some of the recruiting companies that visit include Accenture, Infosys, JP Morgan, etc. Mostly, the roles offered are SDE, business analyst, etc.
content.Infrastructure: The classrooms are nice, and we get Wi-Fi in the classrooms too. I personally love the library, it's just perfect. Hostels are great, to be honest, infrastructure-wise, they can be very strict for girls, though. Few have study rooms even. Food is great, there's so much variety, and it keeps changing every once in a while. A few hostels have a badminton and basketball court very close to them, if you are lucky enough.

admission_overview.college_name: KIIT Admission 2026: Eligibility, Dates, Fees & Selection Process

admission_overview.title: KIIT Admission Overview 2026
admission_overview.description: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology course admission 2026 is mainly entrance-based. The deemed-to-be University conducts KIITEE for admission to most of the UG and PG courses. The university also considers scores in national-level entrance exams such as NATA, JEE Main Paper-2, CAT, MAT, etc. for specific courses. KIIT offers courses at UG, PG and doctoral levels via its 33 schools. The deemed-to-be University offers courses in Engineering, Management, Arts, Science, Commerce and other streams. Candidates can apply online for the preferred course.

eligibility_selection.title: KIIT Eligibility & Selection Criteria 2026
eligibility_selection.description: KIIT admission is based on merit or entrance exam scores of the aspirants. Interested candidates must fulfill the minimum eligibility criteria, followed by the other selection rounds as per the admission process to get their seat at KIIT. The basic KIIT eligibility for UG courses is Class 12 and graduation for PG programmes. Check below popular KIIT courses with their eligibility and selection criteria: Course Name

courses_table.eligibility: Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics with at least 60% aggregate Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics/Biology/Biotechnology with a minimum of 60% aggregate for BTech Biotechnology
Graduation in any discipline with a minimum of 50% aggregate along with 50% aggregate in Class 10 and Class 12 board examination*
courses_table.selection_criteria: KIITEE Management/ CAT/ MAT/ XAT/ CMAT

courses_table.eligibility: Passed in Class 12 in any stream or equivalent with a minimum of 50% aggregate and having Mathematics/ Business Mathematics/ Economics/ Statistics as one of the subjects in Class 12
Class 12 or equivalent examination with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and English with at least 45% aggregate
Bachelor’s degree in any branch of Science/ Agriculture/ Pharmacy/ Veterinary / Engineering / Technology / Medicine (MBBS/BDS) with at least 55% aggregate
Passed Class 12 in any stream with at least 50% aggregate and having Mathematics /Business Mathematics & Statistics as one of the subjects at Class 12 level

faqs.answer: Aspirants must pass Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics with at least 60% aggregate to apply for BTech at Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology.Aspirants must pass Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics/ Biology/ Biotechnology with a minimum of 60% aggregate for BTech in Biotechnology.
faqs.question: Who is eligible for BTech at KIIT?

faqs.answer: Aspirants must complete Class 12 or equivalent examination with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and English with at least 45% aggregate to apply for BSc Nursing programme at Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility for BSc Nursing at KIIT?

paragraphs: KIIT admission is open for 2026 session. Sudents need to appear for the selection rounds as per the admission process to get a seat at KIIT. Aspirants can get into KIIT via its own entrance exam or other accepted exam scores. Check below the steps to get a seat in the university:
1. KIIT Application Process 2026

Aspirants can fill out the form on university's website for the preferred course. Aspirants can also download the form. Candidates need to fill out the form and submit documents to apply for a course.
2. Selection/ Entrance Exam

KIIT conducts KIITEE for admission to all courses except MBBS, BDS, PG Medical and PG Dental. Aspirants with a score in any other accepted entrance exams can get admission. Candidates may also need to appear for other selection rounds for a few courses.
3. Result & Admission Confirmation

KIIT releases the result of the entrance test online. Selected candidates are called for document verification. Aspirants also need to pay theKIIT feesto confirm their seat. Check below documents required at the time of admission:

courses_data.name: KIIT BTech Admission 2026
eligibility: Accepting Exams:KIITEE, CBSE 12th, ISC, Odisha CHSE
courses_data.description: KIIT offers a four-year BTech course at the UG level. The course is divided into eight semesters. The deemed-to-be University offers BTech in Civil, Construction Technology, Mechanical, Aerospace, Computer Science Engineering, etc. KIIT also offers a five-year Integrated BTech-MTech in Biotechnology course. Aspirants must complete Class 12 with PCM with at least 60% aggregate to apply for BTech.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for BTech Dual Degree at KIIT?
faqs.answer: Aspirants must pass Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics/ Biology/ Biotechnology with a minimum of 60% aggregate to apply for BTech-MTech Dual degree in Biotechnology at Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology.

faqs.question: Does KIIT offer lateral entry in BTech?
faqs.answer: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology does offer lateral entry to BTech programme. Students selected for lateral entry get direct admission in the second year of the course. Candidates must pass a three-year Diploma course in Engineering with a minimum of 60% aggregate from State Council of Technical Education of any state or equivalent to apply for BTech (LE).

courses_data.name: M.E./M.Tech Admissions 2026
eligibility: Accepting Exams:KIITEE, OJEE, GATE, UGC NET

courses_data.name: KIIT MBA Admission 2026
eligibility: Accepting Exams:MAT, XAT, KIITEE, KIITEE Management, CMAT, CAT
courses_data.description: KIIT offers a two-year MBA programme at the PG level. The course is divided into four semesters. KIIT offers General MBA, MBA in Rural Management and MBA in Agribusiness Management. Admission to MBA programme is based on scores of candidates in KIITEE Management, CAT, MAT, CMAT, etc. Aspirants can apply for MBA after completing graduation.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for admission to KIIT MBA?
faqs.answer: Candidates must complete graduation in any discipline with at least 50% aggregate to apply for MBA at KIIT. Aspirants must also have a score in CAT, MAT, CMAT, etc. for admission. Candidates can also appear fo KIITEE Management for admission to MBA programme.

faqs.question: Is CAT required for admission to MBA at KIIT?
faqs.answer: CAT is one of the accepting exams for admission to MBA programme at Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology. Aspirants can also get into MBA via their scores in MAT, CMAT, etc. Candidates can also appear for KIITEE Management conducted by KIIT.

faqs.question: How can I get admission at KIIT?
faqs.answer: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology offers admission to most of the courses based on entrance exam scores of the candidates. The university conducts KIITEE for admission. Aspirants with scores in any other accepted entrance exams can also get admission. Once selected, candidates need to come for document verification and fee payment.

faqs.question: Can I get direct admission at KIIT?
faqs.answer: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology offers admission to most of the courses based on entrance exam. Aspirants need to appear for KIITEE conducted by the university or provide scores in any of the accepted entrance exams for admission. Candidates must contact the admission office to know about the direct admission process.

faqs.question: How many students can do BBA at KIIT School of Management?
faqs.answer: Admission to the MBA is based on the score of CAT or any other entrance exam such as XAT/MAT according to the college and the candidate should have a minimum of 50% in 10+2 grade. It depends on the merit list of the year released by the college every year. The minimum cut-off to get admission in KIIT usually ranges from 92-95% and the score of MAT usually ranges to 80-85%. Candidates can take admission by searching for the merit list on the college portal followed by counseling. Candidates can submit the fees for securing a seat in the college. The students whose score fall under the merit list can pursue MBA from KIIT School of Mangement and the availa bility of seats changes every year.

placement_data.title: KIIT Placement Overview 2026
paragraphs: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology placement season for 2026 is still underway. As per the report, so far 70% students have been placed during the recent placement drive. As of now more than 3500 offers have been made during KIIT Bhubaneswar placements 2026. The highest package offered to the students was INR 44.52 LPA . A total of 272 companies like Infosys, Deloitte, Accenture, Capgemini and many more were a part of the 2026 placements.

highlights.description:
KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology is one of the most popular institutes of the country. Most of the ranking bodies have recognised this institute as one of the popular choices among students. The institute has been ranked #27 by the NIRF 2025 under the category Overall, #17 under University, #36 under Engineering, #68 under Management, and #14 under Law. The institute has been ranked well by eminent ranking bodies because of its curriculum, infrastructure and well-qualified faculty. Students can check the article in this space for more details around the KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology rankings.
Read less

nirf.description:
Based on the rankings from NIRF, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology outperformed numerous other institutes, securing a higher position, indicating that the institute is considered to be doing better in various factors evaluated by the NIRF ranking system. In the 'Overall' category, the institute's ranks have improved in comparison with last year's ranking. In 2025, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology climbed 1 rank to #27 rank in the 'Overall' category by the NIRF ranking. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has ascended in the rankings.
It has been observed that the institute has displayed a fluctuating trend in the 'University' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2025, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology fell 2 ranks to #17 rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking.
In the 'Engineering' category, the institute's ranks have ascended when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2025, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology climbed 1 rank to #36 rank in the 'Engineering' category by the NIRF ranking. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has ascended in the rankings.
The institute's ranks in the 'Law' category; have shown a fluctuation when evaluated against previous year's ranking. In 2025, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology fell 3 ranks to #14 rank in the 'Law' category by the NIRF ranking.
Explore the most recent rankings from other ranking bodies and their respective categories below.
Read less

qs_world.description:
KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology has been recognised for its excellence in various fields by QS World University Rankings 2026, outperforming other institutes in parameters such as Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity & Research, as well as fostering an exceptional learning environment.
It is to be noted that the institute has demonstrated a fluctuating trend in the 'Asia' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2026, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology fell 37 ranks to #294 rank in the 'Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings.
The institute has displayed a fluctuating trend in the 'Southern Asia' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2026, KIIT - Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology fell 12 ranks to #67 rank in the 'Southern Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings.

hostel.description: There are 20 hostels for boys and 11 hostels for girls are available with accommodation capacity of 10180 and 5648 students respectively. About 30% of the hostel rooms are air-conditioned and all the hostels have attached canteens serving good quality food. Each hostel has 24-hour reading room, the facility for indoor and outdoor games and wireless internet and intranet connectivity. There is a dedicated international hostel accommodates foreign students.

library.description:
KIIT central library has successfully built up a comprehensive collection of over 
6,83,616 books covering different branches of knowledge. Besides, there are also more than 1,27,541 books available in electronic form covering varied subjects.The library subscribes to 718 print journals (both Indian and Foreign) specific to the academic and research needs of the academic community of the university. There are more than 28,000 electronic journals and 3.7 million e-theses & dissertations available in full text through the online databases subscribed by the library. There are also 6806 bound volumes journals in the library. 555 educational videos are available for academic consultation in the library. Also holds a collection of VSS reports, MTS reports, Project reports, case studies, working papers, annual reports etc. in all subject areas being taught at the University.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Badminton Court, Basketball Court, Cricket Ground, Football Ground, Swimming Pool, Tennis Court, Hockey Ground, Volleyball Court, Throwball Court, Rugby Ground, Track & Field (400 m)

reviews.review_heading: It has good infrastructure and faculty members.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are good. The library is good. Wi-Fi in hostels is very bad and has very poor coverage. I cannot even video call properly. The mess food is absolute trash, and the food even causes various diseases. The rest of the things are good, like medical facilities, sports, and games.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with the college, hostel, faculty member etc.
reviews.review_text: The classrooms at KSRM are really very good, and the environment is really appreciable. Especially, what I feel is the library of KSRM and the KIIT central library, they are equipped with many options for students to learn on their own, which helps students gain extra knowledge.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Excellent rural exposure, faculty, and strong placement with decent hostel facilities with the best
reviews.review_text: Departments are well-maintained with projectors. Wi-Fi is available across the campus. The library is well-maintained with a full range of books and is well-managed. Hostel rooms are good, decent, and comfortable. Mess food is average but hygiene is maintained which is essential. The canteen is spacious and kept clean and hygienic.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Computer Engineering, Kalinga Institute of Industrial TechnologyReviewed on 2 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Good infrastructure, supportive faculty, and decent placement opportunities.
reviews.review_text: College has good infrastructure for both academics and hostel life. Classrooms are big and well maintained with proper seating and smart teaching facilities. Labs are having updated systems and required software. Wi-Fi is available across the campus. Central library has a wide collection of textbooks, reference books, journals, and digital resources. It helps during exams and projects. Hostel facilities are decent with clean rooms. Along with regular water and electricity supply, and adequate se...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by KIIT School of Computer Engineering, Kalinga Institute of Industrial TechnologyReviewed on 31 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overall, it's a good college.
reviews.review_text: The classrooms are nice, and we get Wi-Fi in the classrooms too. I personally love the library, it's just perfect. Hostels are great, to be honest, infrastructure-wise, they can be very strict for girls, though. Few have study rooms even. Food is great, there's so much variety, and it keeps changing every once in a while. A few hostels have a badminton and basketball court very close to them, if you are lucky enough.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: It has good infrastructure, faculty, and a good track record for placement.
reviews.review_text: This college has good infrastructure and is well-furnished. They serve satisfactory food, and the hostel is in a short distance from the college with student-friendly staff. They also have AC premium hostel, which is much expensive than the regular non-AC hostels. The laundry system of the university can be improved as it lacks the quality of washing and its services.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Perfect for a successful future.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is very good and available 24/7, classrooms are well-maintained, and the library is very good. Every book is available for our reference and future goal achievement. Hostels are very good, and the rooms are ventilated, the mess food is very good, and it provides 4 times meals a day. For medical cases, they take students to KIMS.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Excellent experience with KIIT School of Rural Management.
reviews.review_text: 24/7 Wi-Fi facility. The quality of food provided in the mess is good. The canteen is also available. An AC facility is available in classrooms, hostels, and the library. 24/7 medical facilities are available. Sports events are also organised. Different types of books are available in the library.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Supportive faculty, good academics, and a positive campus atmosphere.
reviews.review_text: The department has good classrooms, Wi-Fi, labs, and a library that support learning. Hostel Facilities are good with clean rooms and basic amenities. The mess food is also good and hygienic. Medical facilities, games, and yoga facilities are provided.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with the excellent faculty and their mentoring of us for our future growth.
reviews.review_text: We have good infrastructure facilities like smart classrooms, a library, a seminar hall, and an auditorium. We have good quality facilities. We have better mess, and in any emergencies, immediate action is taken by the hostel warden.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Good infrastructure, teachers, surroundings, opportunity, and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The campus is fully Wi-Fi enabled, with smart classes, a seminar hall, an auditorium, and a library, which is open throughout the week. The hostel is well-maintained with basic amenities and security. Mess is of good quality and has a variety of food options. The canteen offers additional options that are inside the campus. 24/7 medical support, sports, games, and activities include gym, swimming, indoor sports, badminton, cricket, and basketball.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by KIIT School of Rural Management (KSRM)
reviews.review_heading: Strong learning impact.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des in Fashion Design - Batch of 2019
individual_reviews.title: The college has good faculty, infrastructure is good but the campus area is very small.

content.Placements: The college guarantees 100% placement to all the students. The college has average packaged companies or designers for recruitment. The students get placed to work under known designers and gain experience. The roles offered in the courses are of as a designer, social media influencer and so.
content.Infrastructure: The college has a very modernised infrastructure with fully equipped security facility to centralised air-conditioning and lift facility. The college does not have its own hostel but the canteen food and medical facilities are present. The college is a fashion and technology college, so has less opportunity for sports and games.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des in Fashion Design - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: Overall performance of our college.

content.Placements: According to the college, there was 97 percent placement but we don't if that is the exact percentage. Seniors said that some Pearl pass out designers also come to take the interview. They don't provide salary as per expectation. Seniors are not happy with their placement. They want good placement.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good. Completely air conditioned. All the classrooms and labs are maintained. Earlier, the food of the canteen was not of a good quality but now they have a variety and good quality food. Stationary is also good, sells goods at a reasonable price. Stairs need to be maintained and lift too. Should have cross ventilation. We have a think tank where we work.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des in Fashion Design - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Our college is the biggest hub for fashion and design.

admission_process.title: Pearl Academy New Friends Colony Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: Pearl Academy, New Friends Colony admissions2025 have started. Candidates can pursue UG, PG and Professional Certificates in several specialisations at the campus. These are BDes, BBA, MBA, MDes, etc.Pearl Academy, New Friends Colony, admission is conducted in several cycles, and candidates can participate in all cycles. As per Pearl Academy admission process, candidates need to clear the Pearl Academy Entrance Exam, popularly known as the PAF Entrance Exam, followed by a Personal Interview (PI). PAF Entrance Exam consists of a General Proficiency Test (GPT) and a Design Aptitude Test (DAT). Students are allotted campuses according to their rank in the entrance test and the preferences filled in the application form.
Pearl Academy 2025 Admissions Open:

PA, NFC course admissionis completely merit-based & Entrance exam-based. Students seeking to apply for any of the courses offered at the college must fulfil the admission criteria of the desired programme before applying for it. Students can refer to the table below to know about the eligibility and selection criteria for some of thePearl Academy, New Friends Colony courses:

Pearl New Friends Colony fees are cumulative of multiple components such as tuition fee, refundable security deposit, registration fee, and alumni fee. An 18% GST is also included in the one-time fee payment. Candidates can check out the table below for the course-wise tuition fees:
NOTE:The above-mentioned fee is taken from an unofficial source. Hence, is indicative.

For admission to Pearl Academy New Friends Colony, candidates have to go through each of the stages of the admission procedure. The process involves online registration, entrance exams, selection rounds, and admission confirmation. More details about each of the stages in the admission process are explained below:
STEP 1: Pearl AcademyNew Friends ColonyApplication Process 2025

Candidates seeking admission at Pearl Academy New Friends Colony must fill out and submit a duly filled application form, provided they have met the basic eligibility criteria prescribed by the institute. The application fee for Bachelor's and Master's degree programmes is INR 2,000, and for Professional Certificate Courses is INR 500. Candidates can read more about thePearl Academy Application Form 2025to understand the procedure better.
STEP 2: Shortlisting of Students

Candidates who register successfully need to take the entrance test in the next step. ThePearl Academy Exam Resultsare published on the Pearl Academy's official website. Additionally, individual scorecards are also emailed to the students. The candidates are shortlisted based on their scores in the accepted entrance exams, like the Pearl Academy Entrance Exam.
STEP 3: Personal Interview
Shortlisted candidates must appear for the interview round.
STEP 4: Payment of Admission Fee & Admission Confirmation
Selected candidates must pay thePearl Academy NFC feeto confirm their admission.

faqs.question: Can I get direct admission to Pearl Academy, New Friends Colony?
faqs.answer: No, direct admission is not possible for UG & PG courses at Pearl Academy, New Friends Colony. Candidates seeking admission to any of the courses at Pearl Academy must qualify for the Pearl Academy Exam, which consists of two rounds. Admission to all UG & PG courses is done through a common selection process. Further, admission to Certificate courses is done through a PI round. It's mandatory for candidates to check the eligibility criteria and qualify the admission process.

faqs.question: Is there any entrance exam for Pearl Academy?
faqs.answer: The Pearl Academy conducts entrance exams for admissions. The admission process is purely entrance-based. These exams such as Pearl Academy Entrance Exam (PAF). PAF Entrance Exam consists of GPT (General Proficiency Test) and that (Design Aptitude Test). Candidates who want this Pearl Academy admission to UG Diploma or PG Diploma courses have to clear the test.

faqs.question: Can I join Pearl Academy New Friends Colony directly?
faqs.answer: No, Pearl Academy New Friends Colony does not offer direct admission. Admission to Pearl Academy's UG and PG courses is entrance-based. To get admission to BDes at Pearl Academy New Friends Colony, candidates must qualify for PAF, which is an institute-level entrance exam.

faqs.question: What entrance exam scores does Pearl Academy New Friends Colony accept for admission to MBA?
faqs.answer: Pearl Academy in New Friends Colony, Delhi accepts scores from the UCEED exam for all design courses.Pearl Academy's entrance exam, the Pearl Academy Entrance Exam (PAF), consists of the General Proficiency Test (GPT) and the Design Aptitude Test (DAT). The selection process also includes a personal interview and portfolio presentation.Pearl Academy's MBA programme has a two-year duration and specializations in fashion and lifestyle business management, and global luxury brand management. The selection criteria for the MBA programme is merit in the GPT and a personal interview.

labs.description: Available Facilities: Computer Lab, photography laboratory, photography studio, dark room, make-up studio, resource center, Fashion Design Studio, Pattern Making and Draping Lab, Garment Construction Lab, Textile Lab, Fashion Styling Lab, Design Studio, Makers’ Lab, Mac Lab, Wacom Lab

reviews.review_heading: The college has good faculty, infrastructure is good but the campus area is very small.
reviews.review_text: The college has a very modernised infrastructure with fully equipped security facility to centralised air-conditioning and lift facility. The college does not have its own hostel but the canteen food and medical facilities are present. The college is a fashion and technology college, so has less opportunity for sports and games.

reviews.review_heading: Overall performance of our college.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is good. Completely air conditioned. All the classrooms and labs are maintained. Earlier, the food of the canteen was not of a good quality but now they have a variety and good quality food. Stationary is also good, sells goods at a reasonable price. Stairs need to be maintained and lift too. Should have cross ventilation. We have a think tank where we work.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is amazing to be in.
reviews.review_text: Our college has huge classrooms and labs all over with high-speed Wi-Fi connectivity. The college building is fully air-conditioned. Our college has a maximum of its walls portrait with graphites. Our college has various rooms for all kinds of different activities related to the course like stitching room, photoshoot, stationery room, etc.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is extremely good, but it is a bit high on the fee.
reviews.review_text: The campus has a building of 4 floors with all the courses on each floor. Walls had graffiti designs all over, and not even a single wall was plain or simple. All kinds of artwork were displayed on every corner and wall of the building. Furniture was also colourful.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is the biggest hub for fashion and design.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is good. Labs are very good, and the availability of products is maximum with appropriate quality. Canteen and other stationery items are sufficient. We have all types of machines needed for our projects. Collaborations and occasions are very helpful. Events are very helpful, and people who come to give knowledge are also of some level. Our college helps to improve our projects. Books are available in the library, which is very good.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is growing with good facilities and resources.
reviews.review_text: The quality of classrooms and labs are quite maintained and hygienic. The cafeteria serves a wide variety of food options at a reasonable price. Students also have access to E-library. The infrastructure of our college is beautiful.
